title: Track D: receipt_writer_completion_evidence_repair route wired to a registered machine owner
author: claude-code-noos (builder)
verdict: SUBMITTED for independent verification — no PASS/DONE claim by the author (author != verifier)

precondition_disclosure.stated_precondition: run after PR #72 and PR #73 have merged to main
precondition_disclosure.actual_state: PR #72 (nf-noos-observability-semantics-001) and PR #73 (nf-noos-motor-registry-hardening-001) were still OPEN at build time; merge-to-main is founder-gated and was NOT performed by the machine.
precondition_disclosure.resolution: Built on branch nf-noos-motor-route-wiring-001 created from origin/main with both PR branches merged in cleanly (no conflicts). The PR for this work is stacked on #72/#73: once they merge, its diff collapses to this task's changes only.

what_was_built.tests: tests/test_noos_motor_route_owner_v1.py (23 tests, network-free, incl. recovery gate + L13 idempotency + validator round-trip)

first_repair_run.kind: LIVE run against a real recurrence, not a synthetic dry-run
first_repair_run.live_condition: At 2026-07-17T08:13Z all 7 loops showed dispatch FRESH (0.9-12m) while completion evidence was STALE (last writer-B rows 07:00:45-47Z, ~72-81m old) — the exact DISPATCHING_COMPLETION_UNPROVEN class from the 2026-07-16/17 incident.

before.source: diagnosis receipt (BEFORE of the external pair)

first_repair_run.repair: One keyed, provenance-labeled evidence-sink row (noetfield_factory_cycle_runs cycle 1330, HTTP 201, attempt 1, cloud_trigger=noos_motor_receipt_writer_repair, repair_key embedded). Same surface as the sanctioned integrator-repair move, made keyed + duplicate-proof.
first_repair_run.wait: one full loop-inbox cycle (interval 5m; waited 6.5m) per recipe capture_after

after.source: observer read path (autorun_status_v1.supabase_get), NOT the writer's code path
after.provenance_disclosed: AFTER row is the labeled repair row (cloud_trigger=noos_motor_receipt_writer_repair) — synthetic green disclosed, never presented as organic writer-B output

checks.no_execution_mutation: PASS — action-log audit: mutation surfaces used = {read_only, evidence_sink, sandbox_filesystem}; zero restarts/redeploys/loop-logic changes
checks.idempotency: PASS — sink scan for repair_key: 1 row; jobs with idempotency_key: 1; live re-run of the repair phase returned DUPLICATE_SUPPRESSED with no second write (L13)

first_repair_run.recovery: states.recovery = PROVEN, only from the external before/after pair (validate.py rejects recovery PROVEN without an external PASS row)
first_repair_run.lifecycle_status: POST_VERIFYING — deliberately NOT RECEIPT_COMPLETE: recipe post-promotion checks (2 consecutive ORGANIC cron completion receipts; manual green != cron green) remain pending

founder_gates_honored.no_verifier_change: motor/registry/validate.py, schemas, recipes, registry tests, scripts/verify_* untouched (L5)
founder_gates_honored.escalation_route: If the pair had FAILED, the owner writes a founder-decision escalation artifact and stops — implemented and unit-tested; it never falls back to restarting execution
founder_gates_honored.pure_keyed_retries_gate: none required per recipe human_gate ('pure keyed retries need no gate'); no writer-fix PR was needed

verification_evidence.validate_py: python3 motor/registry/validate.py -> exit 0 (recipes: 5, jobs: 2, v1.2 fail-closed invariants)
verification_evidence.tests: 99 passed: motor/registry/tests (52) + test_noos_motor_route_owner_v1 (33, incl. 10 adversarial-review regressions) + test_noos_observability_semantics_v1 (14)
verification_evidence.full_suite: pytest -q: 255 passed (CI parity: requirements.txt deps only)
verification_evidence.adversarial_review: 28-agent workflow (4 review dimensions -> 24 findings -> 24 adversarial verifiers): 21 confirmed, 3 refuted. All 21 confirmed findings fixed, each with a regression test where testable. Highlights fixed: (critical) honest-failure job records were schema-invalid (FAILED verification without reason, I2) — both failure paths now round-trip the frozen validator; (critical) recovery could be claimed PROVEN after a dry-run that merely observed recovery — recovery now additionally requires the repair to have actually been performed, with explicit attribution wording; (major) any failed check now routes to TRIAGE_REQUIRED + escalation artifact (on_check_fail=escalate), not a green POST_VERIFYING; (major) keyed retry re-checks the sink between attempts so an ambiguous network failure cannot double-write (L13); (major) network errors no longer crash the run or leak the sandbox (try/finally + typed exception handling); (major) job files are never silently overwritten across different idempotency keys, and the complete phase refuses a mismatched --job-id and a missing run log with BLOCKED_WITH_REASON; (minor) BEFORE now carries the last-good row's provenance; complete phase writes a separate receipt file (append-only control plane); registry README Track D section updated to WIRED.

l13_idempotency_design: Job level: jobs-dir scan on idempotency_key rendered from the recipe dedupe_key (receipt-repair:{loop_id}:{diagnosis_id}) — same key never creates a second job. Sink level: pre-write existence check on runner_output->>repair_key — same key never writes a second row. Both were exercised live.
secret_scope_disclosure: The recipe declares secret_scope [sink_receipts_write_token]. A receipts-table-scoped token is not yet provisioned in this environment; run #1's single evidence-sink write used the same platform credentials the sanctioned integrator-repair path uses (~/.noetfield-platform-secrets, service role). Deviation disclosed, not hidden. The owner now prefers NOOS_SINK_RECEIPTS_WRITE_TOKEN when set and records which credential was used in its action log; provisioning the scoped token is follow-up hardening.
run1_code_version_disclosure: Run #1's artifacts (job record, single run-log file receipts/motor/noos-motor-route-owner-run-NOOS-DIAG-20260717T082142Z-inbox.json) were produced by the pre-review code version, which rewrote the run log in place between phases (a deviation from control_plane_receipts: append_only, confirmed by the adversarial review). The artifacts are kept unmodified as the honest record of that run; the fixed code writes separate -repair/-complete receipt files for all future runs. Run #1's record content itself was verified accurate (its AFTER row WAS the keyed repair row, so its recovery attribution holds).

post_check_and_escalation.post_check: receipts/motor/noos-motor-post-check-20260717T084916Z-inbox.json — decision FAIL (honest): latest 2 completion receipts are both repair-labeled, 0 organic. The job record therefore remains POST_VERIFYING, never RECEIPT_COMPLETE.
post_check_and_escalation.founder_decision_escalation: receipts/motor/noos-motor-founder-decision-organic-writerB-20260717T0850Z.json — persistent organic writer-B silence (3rd occurrence of the class) likely needs cloud-runner investigation/redeploy, which is founder_required. Machine stopped and escalated; execution was never restarted.

not_claimed: Organic writer-B recovery (post-check ran and honestly FAILED: 2 consecutive cron-triggered organic completion receipts not yet observed)
Root cause of the cloud writer-B silence (not locally establishable; isolation bounded to sandbox smoke + observer reads; escalated to founder decision)
Any PASS/DONE verdict — this receipt is builder output, SUBMITTED for independent verification
